summary: Global equity markets are experiencing a broadly risk-on mood, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq both closing at record highs as easing geopolitical tensions and strong AI-driven earnings lifted investor sentiment. The dominant macro catalyst is progress toward a US-Iran nuclear and trade deal, with Trump signaling productive talks, Iran reviewing a new US proposal, and both sides reportedly closing in on an agreement, driving Europe's STOXX 600 up over 2% and UK stocks rising 2% on the optimism. AI stocks led sector gains after AMD's strong results sparked a broad rally, while Boeing and Honeywell surged on separate catalysts, and Warner Bros. Discovery weighed on media after booking a $2.9 billion net loss tied to its Paramount deal and restructuring charges. The primary risk factor investors are monitoring is the fragility of the Strait of Hormuz and Gulf shipping lanes, underscored by a CMA CGM vessel being struck in the strait, France deploying an aircraft carrier to the Red Sea, and data showing airline jet fuel costs surging 56% following the Iran conflict's outbreak. In the short term, the market's trajectory hinges heavily on whether a formal US-Iran deal materializes, with a confirmed agreement likely to further compress oil risk premiums and sustain the equity rally, while any breakdown in negotiations or additional Hormuz shipping incidents could rapidly reverse the current gains.
